title: Danthonia melanathera
taxonomy: Danthonia melanathera is classified in the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the monocotyledonous flowering plants. The genus Danthonia itself is a part of the tribe Danthonieae and subfamily Danthonioideae. This classification further emphasizes the plant's connection to other grasses. Further studies may be needed to determine the exact relationships within the family and genus.

intro: Danthonia melanathera, also known as Black Needle Grass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
